item.title: Avionics Technician- 14x14 - Houma, LA
item.description: Bristow Group is continuously seeking top talent to join our team. Applications are now being accepted for Avionics Technicians to provide support to bases located in the Gulf of Mexico. Applications are reviewed based on business needs and individual qualifications. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals and with provisions of the Federal Air Regulations; Properly signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs as described in the RSM; Owning and maintaining a basic set of standard and metric aircraft quality tooling sufficient or specific for the shop or floor area in which he/she is assigned; Properly and efficiently using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials; If work is to continue to a following shift, providing a detailed and complete report of work progression for tie in with his/her relief; Keeping up to date with manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to life or property; Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment; Ensuring the Shop Lead or designee is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). Performing other duties as assigned.

item.title: Aviation Maintenance Technician - 14x14 - Houma, LA
item.description:
$32.00–$46.00 USD DOE
The A&P Field‑Based Helicopter Aviation Maintenance Technician is responsible for ensuring Bristow aircraft are maintained in a safe, airworthy, and mission‑ready condition in full compliance with all applicable regulatory requirements, airworthiness directives, and Bristow Group maintenance policies and procedures. Duties include maintaining close coordination with the Lead Aviation Maintenance Technician, performing inspection, maintenance, modification, and repair of Bristow rotor‑wing aircraft, planning and scheduling maintenance activities, communicating with pilots and operational crews, maintaining the field base environment, managing parts and inventory, and completing all required aircraft records and maintenance documentation.

item.title: Apprentice - Paint Tech
item.description: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals and with provisions of the Federal Air Regulations; Properly signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs as described in the RSM; Properly and efficiently using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials; If work is to continue to a following shift, providing a detailed and complete report of work progression for tie in with his/her relief; Keeping up to date with manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct; Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to life or property; Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment; Ensuring the Shop Lead or designee, is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Read and understand drawings, schematics, aircraft manuals, etc; Motivated, Self-Starter capable of maintaining an efficient work pace; Be able to work both as part of a team as well as independently; Be flexible as this job may require travel to include International as needed; Be able to work overtime as required; Willing to relocate to Lake Charles; Two (2) to Three (3) years Paint experience preferred but not required.

item.title: Pilot - 14x14 - Gulf of America
item.description: Bristow Group is continuously seeking top talent to join our team. Applications are now being accepted for VFR and IFR PICs, as well as IFR SIC helicopter pilots to provide support to the offshore oil & gas industry. All qualified applicants can submit an application and resume by applying to the link below. Applications are reviewed based on business needs and individual qualifications. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Operates their assigned aircraft in a safe and efficient manner, in strict compliance with Bristow operational standards and all applicable regulations; Places safety and customer service at the forefront in everything they do, both inside and outside the aircraft; Maintains situational awareness throughout the entire operational region with respect to weather conditions, NOTAMs, sea state and customer offshore facility status; Manages passenger movements, aircraft movements, briefings and baggage loading to ensure safety and compliance; Completes necessary flight planning paperwork, preflight, fueling and oversight of manifesting to ensure compliance; Maintains with accuracy both electronic and paper records to include journey logs, personal flight and duty sheets, and aircraft technical logbook times; Reports all aircraft mechanical irregularities by entering them in the aircraft paper and electronic technical log and ensures aircraft is airworthy before, during and after all flights; Facilitates communications with the customer and ensures Bristow is represented in a professional manner; Primarily works in the GOM offshore region, but remains available for dispatch to other locations to include, international assignments in accordance with business needs; Stays up to date on all Bristow operational and safety procedures; and Complies with other duties and assignments as determined by the Lead Pilot / Operations Manager.

item.title: Aviation Maintenance Technician - 145 Repair Station - 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
item.description: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Promoting safety in all areas by ensuring that all instructions pertinent to safety are properly observed. Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals. Signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to personnel or property. Reporting all hazardous practices and equipment to his/her Hangar Lead and/or the Repair Station Manager. Using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials properly and efficiently. Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment. Staying current on manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct. Ensuring the Hangar Lead is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). Performing other duties as assigned. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Valid FAA A&P license required; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Must be available to work overtime as required; Must be able to travel internationally as required; Must possess or be able to possess a valid Passport; Previous training or experience with company specific airframes preferred; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Must be detail orientated, a fast learner, and be a self-starter.

item.title: Pilot (S76 PIC) - 14x14 - Houma, LA
item.description: Bristow Group is continuously seeking top talent to join our team. Applications are now being accepted for IFR PIC helicopter pilots to provide support to the offshore oil & gas industry. All qualified applicants can submit an application and resume by applying to the link below. Applications are reviewed based on business needs and individual qualifications. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Operates their assigned aircraft in a safe and efficient manner, in strict compliance with Bristow operational standards and all applicable regulations; Places safety and customer service at the forefront in everything they do, both inside and outside the aircraft; Maintains situational awareness throughout the entire operational region with respect to weather conditions, NOTAMs, sea state and customer offshore facility status; Manages passenger movements, aircraft movements, briefings and baggage loading to ensure safety and compliance; Completes necessary flight planning paperwork, preflight, fueling and oversight of manifesting to ensure compliance; Maintains with accuracy both electronic and paper records to include journey logs, personal flight and duty sheets, and aircraft technical logbook times; Reports all aircraft mechanical irregularities by entering them in the aircraft paper and electronic technical log and ensures aircraft is airworthy before, during and after all flights; Facilitates communications with the customer and ensures Bristow is represented in a professional manner; Primarily works in the GOM offshore region, but remains available for dispatch to other locations to include, international assignments in accordance with business needs; Stays up to date on all Bristow operational and safety procedures; and Complies with other duties and assignments as determined by the Lead Pilot / Operations Manager.

item.title: BSEE Pilot (PIC) - 14x14 Schedule - Houma, LA
item.description: Bristow Group is continuously seeking top talent to join our team. Applications are now being accepted for VFR helicopter pilots to provide support to the offshore oil & gas industry. This Captain position works a 14 on, 14 off schedule (relief role) in support of contracts, based in Houma, Louisiana. Duties include operating assigned aircraft safely, maintaining situational awareness, managing passenger and aircraft movements, completing flight planning paperwork, and ensuring compliance with operational standards.

item.title: Avionics Technician - 14x14 Shift - Houma, LA
item.description: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals and with provisions of the Federal Air Regulations; Properly signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs as described in the RSM; Owning and maintaining a basic set of standard and metric aircraft quality tooling sufficient or specific for the shop or floor area in which he/she is assigned; Properly and efficiently using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials; If work is to continue to a following shift, providing a detailed and complete report of work progression for tie in with his/her relief; Keeping up to date with manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to life or property; Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment; Ensuring the Shop Lead or designee is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). Performing other duties as assigned.

item.title: Aviation Maintenance Technician - 28x28 - Suriname
item.description: Applications are now being accepted for 135 Aviation Maintenance Technicians to provide support to the offshore oil & gas industry. 28x28 Rotational Shift. Travel Covered. Housing Provided. Per Diem. Other contract related benefits. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals. Ensuring all aircraft are maintained to the highest airworthy and cosmetic standards and always reflect company standards. Signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to personnel or property. Reporting all hazardous practices and equipment to his/her Lead Aviation Maintenance Technician. Performing proficient and timely receiving inspections on incoming direct ship parts, stock transfer receipts and core parts handling when at a base without supply personnel. Using all Company tools, parts, equipment, and materials properly and efficiently. Communicating daily with the assigned flight crews with respect to the aircraft condition and ongoing maintenance to ensure the highest level of teamwork and visibility is achieved. Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment. Maintaining a clean work area during and after all job functions; Providing on-the-job instruction and supervision of personnel assigned to assist him/her. Staying current on manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct; and Performing other duties as assigned.

item.title: Mechanic I – Brazil
item.description: We are looking for a Helicopter Mechanic I to perform preventive and corrective maintenance under supervision, ensuring the safety and airworthiness of our fleet, in compliance with ANAC regulations, manufacturers’ manuals, and Bristow’s core values. Key responsibilities include carrying out inspections, repairs, and scheduled/unscheduled maintenance under supervision, performing pre-flight, between-flight, and post-flight inspections according to technical manuals, ensuring compliance with the Aircraft Maintenance Program, and keeping the workplace clean, organized, and safe.

item.title: Avionics Technician- 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
item.description: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals and with provisions of the Federal Air Regulations; Properly signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs as described in the RSM; Owning and maintaining a basic set of standard and metric aircraft quality tooling sufficient or specific for the shop or floor area in which he/she is assigned; Properly and efficiently using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials; If work is to continue to a following shift, providing a detailed and complete report of work progression for tie in with his/her relief; Keeping up to date with manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to life or property; Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment; Ensuring the Shop Lead or designee is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). Performing other duties as assigned. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Read and understand drawings, schematics, aircraft manuals, etc; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Motivated, Self-Starter capable of maintaining an efficient work pace; Be able to work both as part of a team as well as independently; Be able to work overtime as required; Previous training or experience with company specific rotary-wing Airframes highly preferred; Three (3) to five (5) years Avionics/Electrical experience required.

item.title: Aviation Maintenance Technician - 145 Repair Station - 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
item.description: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Promoting safety in all areas by ensuring that all instructions pertinent to safety are properly observed. Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals. Signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to personnel or property. Reporting all hazardous practices and equipment to his/her Hangar Lead and/or the Repair Station Manager. Using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials properly and efficiently. Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment. Staying current on manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct. Ensuring the Hangar Lead is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). Performing other duties as assigned. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Valid FAA A&P license required; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Must be available to work overtime as required; Must be able to travel internationally as required; Must possess or be able to possess a valid Passport; Previous training or experience with company specific airframes preferred; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Must be detail orientated, a fast learner, and be a self-starter.

item.title: Avionics Technician- 14x14 - Houma, LA
item.description: Bristow Group is continuously seeking top talent to join our team. Applications are now being accepted for Avionics Technicians to provide support to bases located in the Gulf of Mexico. Applications are reviewed based on business needs and individual qualifications. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals and with provisions of the Federal Air Regulations; Properly signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs as described in the RSM; Owning and maintaining a basic set of standard and metric aircraft quality tooling sufficient or specific for the shop or floor area in which he/she is assigned; Properly and efficiently using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials; If work is to continue to a following shift, providing a detailed and complete report of work progression for tie in with his/her relief; Keeping up to date with manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to life or property; Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment; Ensuring the Shop Lead or designee is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). Performing other duties as assigned. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Read and understand drawings, schematics, aircraft manuals, etc; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Motivated, Self-Starter capable of maintaining an efficient work pace; Be able to work both as part of a team as well as independently; Be able to work overtime as required; Previous training or experience with company specific rotary-wing Airframes highly preferred; Three (3) to five (5) years Avionics/Electrical experience preferred.

item.title: Aviation Maintenance Technician - 145 Repair Station- 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
item.description: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Promoting safety in all areas by ensuring that all instructions pertinent to safety are properly observed. Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals. Signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs. Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to personnel or property. Reporting all hazardous practices and equipment to his/her Hangar Lead and/or the Repair Station Manager. Using all Company tools, parts, equipment and materials properly and efficiently. Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment. Staying current on manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct. Ensuring the Hangar Lead is immediately aware of aircraft irregularities (i.e. major maintenance discrepancies, project schedule delays, record discrepancies or emergencies). Performing other duties as assigned. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Valid FAA A&P license required; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Must be available to work overtime as required; Must be able to travel internationally as required; Must possess or be able to possess a valid Passport; Previous training or experience with company specific airframes preferred; Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); Must be detail orientated, a fast learner, and be a self-starter.

item.title: Ground Operations Officer
item.description: The main purpose of the Ground Operations Officer is to help and assist the pilots and act as the link between NHV Denmark and the client. Responsibilities include making all the preparation for flights, planning and coordination for all NHV Denmark’s flight operations, understanding the client’s business and needs, liaising with customers on delays and significant events, scheduling pilots, ensuring all administration is carried out to the required standard and regulations, anticipating and coordinating required changes to the flight schedule, assisting colleagues, and complying with Information Security Management System (ISMS) policies.

item.title: Finance Manager
item.description: NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. We are looking for a strategic and hands-on Finance Manager to lead our Group Finance function and strengthen our financial foundations for sustainable growth. Reporting directly to the CFO, the Finance Manager provides strategic and operational leadership across Finance, ensuring strong governance, regulatory compliance, commercial insight, and sustainable performance across the NHV Group. As accountable lead for Group accounting and tax compliance, you will partner closely with the CFO and senior leadership to shape international strategy, manage financial risk, and drive organizational performance. You combine strategic vision with operational excellence, continuously enhancing finance processes, systems, and policies to improve efficiency, transparency, and control.

item.title: AW139 Co-Pilot
item.description: Are you an experienced AW139 Co-Pilot ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential. As an AW139 Co-Pilot, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. Your role includes flight preparation and execution, compliance with professional standards, and supporting offshore operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot.

item.title: Technical Director
item.description: We are seeking an experienced and strategic Technical Director to lead and oversee all technical helicopter operations across our multi-base organization. Reporting directly to the CEO and serving on the Extended Executive Committee, you will hold full accountability for Maintenance (Part-145), CAMO, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, and Part-147 training. You will ensure safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations while driving performance, cost control, and fleet availability. This is a high-impact executive role combining operational leadership with strong business acumen and full P&L responsibility. You will lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, and sustainability programs, positioning the organization for future growth.

item.title: Flight Operations Officer
item.description: The Flight Operations Officer is responsible for the execution of the daily base flight schedule for our base in Den Helder. They will interface with a number of internal and external stakeholders to manage the safe, compliant and efficient delivery of the program, whilst upholding a high standard of service to NHV’s clients. The position is an operational as well as administrative function, part of the operations department, and reports directly to the Service Delivery Lead. This is a part-time function (20 hours / week). Depending on the candidate and preferences, this could be extended. Responsibilities include Flight Planning & Coordination, Crew Management, Client & Stakeholder Liaison, and Administration & Compliance.

item.title: AW139 Captain
item.description: Are you an experienced AW139 Captain ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential. As an AW139 Captain, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. You report to the Chief Pilot and play a key role in representing the company towards our offshore clients. Your role includes flight preparation and execution, compliance with professional standards, and supporting offshore operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot.

item.title: Experienced HHO Operator – Licensed Engineer
item.description: Are you an experienced Helicopter Hoist Operator and licensed engineer with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights and crew change flights. The helicopter Hoist Operator plays a critical role combining technical skills as a Licensed Line Engineer working on the helicopter and performing hoist flights hoisting passengers to the wind farms. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests.

item.title: B1.3 Engineer AW139
item.description: Are you an experienced EASA Part 66 B1.3 Licensed Engineer ready to work in a dynamic offshore helicopter environment? At our Den Helder base, you will play a key role in keeping our AW139 fleet mission-ready and safe. You’ll join a close-knit team of 8 engineers, working in a professional yet supportive environment where safety, teamwork and technical excellence come first. As a B1.3 Engineer, you are responsible for performing and certifying line maintenance in accordance with approved maintenance data and company procedures. You work closely with fellow engineers under the supervision of the Shift Supervisor, reporting hierarchically to the Base Manager and functionally to the Maintenance Lead. You are more than a certifying engineer — you are a problem solver, safety ambassador and key contributor to operational continuity.

item.title: B1.3 and B2 H175 Engineer
item.description: Are you an experienced licensed engineer on H175 with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled crew change flights and Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights. The helicopter B1.3 or B2 Engineer plays a critical role ensuring the helicopter is ready and fit for flight and is in an airworthy condition. Performing all required scheduled and unscheduled line maintenance. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations for crew changes and hoisting on wind farms. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests.

item.title: H175 Co-Pilot
item.description: To execute scheduled offshore energy flights in the safest and most efficient manner in accordance with aviation regulation and whilst upholding NHV standards. Responsibilities include assisting the Pilot-in-Command with technical preparation of flights, ensuring pre and post flight administration is completed, conducting pilot duties under supervision, maintaining knowledge of NHV Ops Manuals, and promoting team spirit among colleagues.

item.title: Mechanic - Aberdeen
item.description: The Mechanic will work in a team and is responsible for the maintenance of the helicopters. They report to the Chief Engineer of the base. NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. Responsibilities include Line Maintenance and Base Maintenance, ensuring compliance with regulations and timely completion of tasks.

item.title: B2 Engineer
item.description: The helicopter B2 Engineer is responsible for the maintenance of the helicopters. They will work in a team of engineers and reports to the Maintenance Lead.

item.title: Technical Safety and Compliance Manager
item.description: The DK Technical Safety and Compliance Manager is the nominated person for the DK AOC and for all QHSE matters. Reporting to the Accountable Manager, you will shape and maintain the organisations commitment to exceptional quality standards. The role will involve travel to NHV bases and supplier sites will be required. The Technical Safety and Compliance Manager co-operates closely with all organisational subdivisions of NHV. Responsibilities and areas include developing and executing an internal audit plan, collaborating with cross functional teams, executing product and process audits, coaching and training auditees, and daily management of safety cases in the reporting system.

item.title: Helicopter mechanic
item.description: As a Mechanic at our base in Lanvéoc, you'll join a small but highly skilled and enthusiast team, committed to excellence. Responsibilities include maintenance tasks on aircraft and components in accordance with issued work orders, applicable manuals, and approved maintenance data. Activities include disassembly & reassembly, rectifying defects, component replacements, and system adjustments. Ensure all safety regulations and procedures are followed at all times; report safety hazards or mandatory occurrences; follow all MOE procedures; record all performed maintenance accurately; raise defect entries for any findings during inspections; support Licensed Engineers during troubleshooting; maintain a clean, safe, and organized working environment; contribute to flexible planning; and help ensure that planned maintenance is completed within appropriate timeframes.

item.title: B2 Engineer - Lanvéoc
item.description: We’re looking for an experienced B2 engineer to join our dedicated team at our base in Lanvéoc, France. You'll be part of a small but highly skilled and enthusiast team, committed to excellence. Responsibilities & areas Perform base & line maintenance on aircraft and components as per work orders; Perform effective troubleshooting based on pilot reports and system indications; Ensure that all maintenance tasks are carried out, following all approved maintenance data, procedures (MOE), and airworthiness regulations; Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to have a serviceable aircraft that meets the operational need; Accurately certify completed tasks and ensure proper documentation in the work pack; Ensure proper handling, segregation, and documentation of removed/installed components; Adhere to safety standards; report hazards and occurrences via the NHV reporting system; Ensure briefing & debriefing of the Chief Engineer and/or Maintenance Lead; Report daily on the maintenance situation; Inform and update the CE/ML on the maintenance situation; Certify B2 category maintenance activities in accordance with the regulations; Work independently when performing in a less controlled environment and take initiative to improve work, show flexibility within the team to complete tasks within appropriate time scales.
